name: People
description: Your full-stack AI people / HR operator for solo founders. One agent that covers hiring, onboarding, performance, compliance, and culture  -  behind one conversation, one context doc, one markdown output folder. No upfront onboarding. Drafts only  -  I never send, schedule, or publish.
author: Houston

agentSeeds..houston/routines/routines.json:
[{"id": "weekly-people-review", "name": "Monday people review", "description": "Every Monday morning: what shipped across hiring, onboarding, performance, compliance, and culture — plus stale drafts and cross-cutting gaps.", "prompt": "Run the Monday people health review. Use the analyze skill with subject=people-health.

Aggregate everything I produced last week from outputs.json, grouped by domain (Hiring, Onboarding, Performance, Compliance, Culture). For each domain, surface (a) what shipped, (b) what's stale — drafts >7 days old, (c) the single most useful next move. Cross-cutting: open-req drift, retention reds with no stay-conversation follow-up, compliance items due in <14 days not closed, review-cycle drift.

Write to analyses/people-health-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md and log in outputs.json. This routine always surfaces — I want the review every Monday even if the week was quiet.", "schedule": "0 8 * * 1", "enabled": true, "suppress_when_silent": false, "created_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z"}, {"id": "weekly-team-pulse", "name": "Friday team pulse", "description": "Friday afternoon: collect this week's 1:1 check-ins so Monday isn't blind on who's quiet or flagged.", "prompt": "Run the weekly team pulse. Use the collect-checkins skill.

Pull the roster from my connected HRIS (or domains.people.roster in the ledger — ask if missing). Send the check-in prompt via my connected Slack channel. Collect responses over the weekend for a Monday summary. Write a dated report to checkins/{YYYY-MM-DD}.md with themes, who's quiet, and flagged responses.

Always surface — a quiet week still matters. A pattern of quiet weeks is the signal.", "schedule": "0 15 * * 5", "enabled": true, "suppress_when_silent": false, "created_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z"}, {"id": "monthly-compliance-audit", "name": "Monthly compliance audit", "description": "1st of every month: I-9 / W-4 / visa renewals, review-cycle drift, policy-refresh dates. Silent unless a new item is due.", "prompt": "Run the monthly compliance audit. Use the compliance-calendar skill.

Full scan of my connected HRIS for work-authorization expirations, I-9 / W-4 completeness, and vesting-cliff dates. Pull the review-cycle rhythm and policy-refresh cadence from context/people-context.md. Update compliance-calendar.md in place and log each substantive change in outputs.json. Compare against the prior audit run.

If nothing new is due in the next 60 days and nothing has drifted since the last audit, end with ROUTINE_OK so this stays silent. Surface only when a new item is due, a document is incomplete, or a visa expiration is approaching. On the first run (no prior audit exists), always surface — that's the baseline.", "schedule": "0 8 1 * *", "enabled": true, "suppress_when_silent": true, "created_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z"}, {"id": "monthly-retention-scan", "name": "Monthly retention scan", "description": "15th of every month: fuse engagement, sentiment, tenure, and comp signals into GREEN/YELLOW/RED. Silent unless a new RED or YELLOW appeared.", "prompt": "Run the monthly retention scan. Use the analyze skill with subject=retention-risk.

Resolve the roster via my connected HRIS. Fuse engagement (last 4 check-ins, Slack activity delta, optional GitHub / Linear / Jira cadence for eng ICs), sentiment (check-in tone drift, cross-team mentions), tenure milestones (cliff vesting, promotion honeymoon, recent manager change), and comp exposure (vs bands in context/people-context.md). Classify GREEN / YELLOW / RED; write the signal combination on every RED.

Write to analyses/retention-risk-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md and log in outputs.json. Compare against the previous month's scan.

If no person's band changed (no new RED, no new YELLOW, no regression), end with ROUTINE_OK so this stays silent. Surface on any new RED / YELLOW or when an existing RED's signal combination changed. On the first run, always surface — that's the baseline. Founder-eyes-only, never public.", "schedule": "0 8 15 * *", "enabled": true, "suppress_when_silent": true, "created_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z"}]
